text.title: Platform Team Charter Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of platform-team-charter-template enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Charter names primary customer (other engineering teams) explicitly — not 'the business'.
rule.rationale: Vague customers make scope decisions political; explicit customers enable product-team-style prioritisation.

rule.statement: Charter ships with an anti-charter: enumerated work the platform team does NOT do.
rule.rationale: Without anti-charter, scope creep is inevitable; the team becomes a help desk for everyone.

rule.statement: Every paved-road service has a documented SLA (availability, support response) and a sunset criterion.
rule.rationale: Services without SLA become unpredictable; without sunset criterion they accumulate as forever-debt.

rule.statement: Support model is tiered: self-service docs → office hours → on-call escalation. No 'just slack the platform team'.
rule.rationale: Unbounded slack-support consumes the team and erodes paved-road work.

rule.statement: Success metrics are platform-health metrics (adoption, lead time, defect rate) — not vanity output metrics.
rule.rationale: Vanity metrics (PRs shipped) reward busywork; health metrics reward outcome.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
